hq: 1825 South Grant Street, Suite 200, San Mateo, CA 94402

system_description.overview: Shiplight is an autonomous AI-driven end-to-end testing and quality assurance platform that helps engineering teams ship software quickly and safely. AI agents navigate web applications like real users to generate, run, and maintain browser-based tests without requiring scripting or direct codebase access. The platform integrates into existing CI/CD pipelines to run tests in parallel at scale.

products.Autonomous Testing Platform: Cloud-based web application used to create, manage, and execute end-to-end browser tests using autonomous AI agents that generate, run, and maintain tests from product flows, documentation, support tickets, and other artifacts.
products.AI-Powered Test Generation and Maintenan: Creates tests from product flows and documentation using multimodal UI understanding. Automatically updates and heals tests as the application changes.
products.Test Execution, Orchestration, and Quali: High-performance test execution infrastructure with parallel runs, detailed test reporting, and debugging views integrated into customer CI/CD pipelines.
products.Implementation and Onboarding Services: Hands-on onboarding and rollout support including initial test strategy, test creation, and CI/CD workflow integration.
products.Ongoing Customer Success and Support: Ongoing support via shared Slack channels and other communication methods.

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; no physical servers. All data processed on cloud services.
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (DR plan spins up multiple servers across all availability zones in AWS; network diagram shows private subnet shared, prod, and staging)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Three private subnets: shared, prod, and staging. ECS Fargate containers in each subnet behind Application Load Balancer. NAT gateway for outbound traffic.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS in conjunction with security policy network settings.
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host with VPN for remote access.
network_architecture.tls: TLS with certificates from an established third-party certificate authority for all web-based system communications.
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet via content filtering system routed through proxy server.
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> VPC -> Application Load Balancer -> Private subnets (shared, prod, staging) -> ECS Fargate containers -> NAT Gateway -> Internet (outbound)

application_architecture.pattern: Microservices (ECS Fargate containers, multiple environments visible in network diagram)
application_architecture.environments: At least 3: shared, prod, staging (visible in network diagram)

databases: Not specifically named — uses cloud database services on AWS
caching: Intelligent caching mentioned for test execution infrastructure
data_storage.backup_frequency: Not specifically disclosed — backup policy exists

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS (password policy via AWS; Active Directory mentioned)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform. MFA required for access to source code management tool.
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Passwords controlled through AWS password policy including periodic forced changes, password expiry, and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited failed logon attempts.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool with MFA-restricted access, logged commits)
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not named (integrated into customer CI/CD pipelines; internal CI/CD implied)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to production requires additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configs with rollback capability; infrastructure as code implied ("baseline configurations are retained within the infrastructure as a code")
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO manages automatic patch management with monthly reviews. Critical/security patches applied as available. Workstations scanned daily for patch compliance. Vulnerability scans performed monthly.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tool with predefined threshold alerts)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named (logging enabled for admin activities, logon attempts, data deletions, config changes, permissions, and roles)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events.

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure, data center services, compute (ECS Fargate), networking, storage

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection installed on all production servers (continuous scanning) and Mac-based workstations/laptops. Virus definitions updated weekly on production servers, daily on workstations.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications. Self-assessment scan prior to every production release.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party.

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to data center facilities; AWS responsible for physical security
A1.2 - Environmental protections; Shiplight does not have an office, managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity criteria - determined not relevant to the system
Privacy criteria - determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 - Branch protection: code repository branch rules require additional approval for every merge to production
CC6.2.2 - Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged users
CC4.1.1 - Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC7.1.2 - Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications
CC6.1.1 - MFA enforced for administrative access to production platform
CC6.1.5 - Cloud provider KMS used for at-rest encryption; production access keys restricted
CC7.4.1 - BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually with annual scenario testing

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-AZ AWS deployment with ECS Fargate, segmented VPCs, ALB, NAT gateway — solid cloud-native setup. Three environments (shared, prod, staging) visible in network diagram. However, single region only and no CDN or multi-region failover. Region not disclosed.

application_architecture.rationale: Container-based microservices on ECS Fargate with multiple environments and branch protection. However, languages, frameworks, and API style not disclosed. Intelligent caching mentioned but database types unnamed.

data_layer.rationale: Backup policy exists, multi-AZ storage implied, per-tenant segregation and data classification/retention policies stated. However, specific database technologies, backup frequency, and storage services not named.

security_posture.rationale: Strong controls: IDS/IPS, annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, MFA for admin access, quarterly access reviews, antivirus on all systems, firewalls, KMS encryption, log protection, server hardening. Weakness: WAF not mentioned, SIEM not named.

devops_maturity.rationale: ECS Fargate container orchestration, IaC-style baseline configs with rollback, branch protection with required approvals, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews. CI/CD tooling and source control tool names not disclosed.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists with annual review and annual testing across different loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover explicitly described (spinning up servers across all AZs). No RTO/RPO disclosed. No multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only AWS named as a subservice organization. No other vendors explicitly named in the report. Single critical infrastructure dependency with no named diversification.

overall.rationale: Well-designed AWS-native security posture for an early-stage company with strong access controls, annual pen testing, quarterly reviews, and BCDR. Significant gaps in disclosed specifics: no database names, no CI/CD tool names, no source control named, no CDN, single region, no WAF, high AWS concentration. Type 1 report means controls only designed, not yet operationally tested.

red_flags: SOC 2 Type 1 only — controls designed but not yet operationally tested over a period
Single cloud provider (AWS) with no named backup or diversification
No WAF mentioned despite web-facing application
Source control and CI/CD tool names not disclosed
yellow_flags: No CDN mentioned for a globally-accessed testing platform
SIEM tool not named — logging exists but no named SIEM
green_flags: Clean network architecture with network diagram showing VPC, segmented private subnets (shared/prod/staging), ALB, ECS Fargate, NAT Gateway
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
MFA enforced for admin access and source code repository
Branch protection with required approvals on every merge to production
IaC-style baseline configs with rollback capability
Access revoked within 1 business day on termination
BCDR tested annually across multiple scenarios
Anti-virus on production servers with weekly definition updates
key_observations: The network diagram explicitly shows 3 separate ECS Fargate environments (shared, prod, staging) within the same VPC — unusual to expose staging in the same diagram, suggests immature environment separation despite the controls language

The product is itself a test automation platform integrating into customer CI/CD pipelines — security of the CI/CD integration path and read access to customer applications is a unique supply-chain risk not addressed in the report
Auditor is Accorp Partners (PAC-FIRM-LIC-47383) — a small boutique SOC 2 firm, not a Big 4 or top-tier auditor
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria explicitly excluded — notable for a platform that reads and generates test data from customer applications
The report is a Type 1 as of October 23, 2025 — very recently attained; Type 2 will be the meaningful milestone
